page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the processor delivers modest performance. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier, with single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 10%. However, the instance shows moderate capability in specific workloads; Passmark CPU scores, including extended instructions and encryption tests, reach average levels. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks also performs in the middle 50% tier, though small-block cached read bandwidth and overall Passmark memory scores remain in the bottom 25%.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is configured for compute-oriented workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Qualitative cost efficiency is aligned with its legacy Intel Xeon hardware profile, offering dedicated CPU resources at a lower entry point compared to newer architecture classes, though at the expense of raw single-threaded speed. Given the weak database, Redis, and web serving benchmarks, this instance is best suited for basic batch processing, utility computing, or lightweight distributed tasks rather than high-throughput database operations or modern large language model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this instance primarily in the lower tiers, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics generally falling in the bottom 25%, though it maintains average performance in Passmark encryption and large-block memory bandwidth tests. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a budget-friendly option for dedicated compute resources where high memory capacity is not required. It is best suited for basic batch processing, legacy application hosting, and lightweight utility workloads rather than high-throughput databases or demanding real-time web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, offering entry-level compute performance for basic utility workloads.

tagline: An entry-level, dedicated x86 server featuring a one-to-two processor-to-memory ratio for basic compute and utility workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on KVM hypervisor
Average performance in Passmark encryption and large-block memory tests
Best suited for basic batch processing and lightweight utility tasks
